title: Short-Form Video Editor (Gaming/TikTok/VRChat Style)
description:
About Us:

We are StageTime, a fast-growing social VR app (similar to VRChat or Rec Room) where users hang out, dress up, and host live events. We have a passionate community, but we need someone to help us show the world how fun and chaotic our game is.



The Role:

We are looking for a highly skilled video editor who understands Gen-Z gaming culture and internet humour. Your primary job will be taking raw VR gameplay footage (events, funny moments, chaotic interactions) and turning it into highly engaging, fast-paced short-form content.



What You Will Do:



Edit highly engaging short-form videos (TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels) to be posted every day.



Cut a new, high-energy 30-second trailer for our Meta Quest Store page to grab attention in the first 3 seconds.



Scrub through raw VR footage from our community events to find the funniest "hook" moments.



The Style We Need:



Fast-paced, high-retention editing (Gorilla Tag, VRChat, or Rec Room style).



Dynamic, animated subtitles tracking avatar movements.



Smart use of zooms, sound effects, and trending audio to emphasize comedic timing.



VR Headset Ownership (Highly Desirable):

Ideally, you own a Meta Quest 2/3 or a PC VR headset. While we will provide you with a weekly folder of organic, funny clips from our live events, having your own headset is a massive bonus. It allows you to log into StageTime yourself to record cinematic B-roll, capture high-res shots of our new cosmetics, or grab specific UI demonstrations whenever you need them for an edit.



How to Apply (READ CAREFULLY):

Do not send a generic cover letter. We will ignore it.

To apply, start your message with the word "STAGE" so we know you read this. Then, include:



A link to your portfolio specifically showing gaming or highly-edited short-form content.



A quick note letting us know if you currently own a VR headset (and which one).



We will select our top candidates and pay you $20 for a quick 30-second trial edit using our raw footage to see if you can match our vibe. The winner gets the full-time role!

title: Experienced Bookkeeper (QuickBooks + Dext Expert)
description:
We are hiring an experienced Bookkeeper on behalf of a Canada-based professional bookkeeping firm that supports growing businesses with clean, accurate, and compliant financials.



This is not an entry-level role.



We are looking for someone who truly understands bookkeeping inside and out—someone who can jump into QuickBooks and Dext confidently, work independently, and maintain high standards with minimal supervision.



If you love clean books, reconciliations, and structured workflows—and you take pride in accuracy—this role is for you.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

- Full-cycle bookkeeping using QuickBooks Online

- Daily and monthly transaction categorization

- Bank and credit card reconciliations

- Managing receipts and documents using Dext

- Reviewing, matching, and validating expenses

- Identifying discrepancies and resolving issues proactively

- Supporting month-end and year-end bookkeeping tasks

- Maintaining organized, audit-ready financial records

- Communicating clearly with the internal team when clarification is needed



What We’re Looking For (Must-Haves)

- Minimum 5 years of hands-on bookkeeping experience

- Expert-level QuickBooks Online knowledge

- Strong experience using Dext 

- Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

- Strong understanding of bookkeeping best practices

- Ability to work independently and manage your own workload

- Reliable internet connection and availability during PST business hours

- Professional written and verbal English communication



Nice-to-Haves (Assets)

- Experience supporting Canadian businesses

- Experience working with accounting firms or bookkeeping agencies

- Familiarity with month-end close processes

- Comfortable working with multiple client files



Why This Role Is a Great Opportunity

- Stable, long-term role with a professional Canadian firm

- Clear processes and expectations

- Opportunity to increase hours based on performance

- Work with a team that values accuracy, accountability, and professionalism

- No micromanagement—your expertise is trusted



How to Apply

- Please include the following in your application:

- A brief summary of your bookkeeping experience

- Your years of experience with QuickBooks Online

- Your experience level with Dext (be specific)

- Examples of bookkeeping tasks you’ve handled independently

- Your availability during PST hours



Only applicants with relevant bookkeeping experience will be considered.

title: Social Media Manager (Mortgage Broker) – Video Editing Rockstar – Part-Time (20 hrs/week, EST)
description:
L’EVATE is hiring on behalf of a successful Mortgage Broker in Ontario, Canada. We’re looking for a high-performing Social Media Manager with strong video editing skills who can take ownership of content execution and help us build a consistent, professional, high-trust online presence.



This role is for someone who can take long-form content (Zoom recordings, interviews, walkthroughs, webinars) and turn it into scroll-stopping short-form videos and posts that actually get watched — plus create marketing materials and resources that support the business.



SCHEDULE (REQUIRED)

• 20 hours/week

• Monday–Friday, 11:00am–3:00pm EST



WHAT YOU’LL DO

• Own the content calendar and ensure consistent posting

• Edit short-form videos for Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts (and
 as needed)

• Repurpose long-form videos into multiple high-quality clips with strong hooks, captions, and clean pacing

• Create supporting social content (posts, carousels, stories) aligned to weekly themes

• Create marketing materials and resources such as flyers, simple brochures/one-pagers, client guides/checklists, event promos, and branded graphics

• Keep assets organized and on-brand (clean naming, folders, versions)

• Track what performs and improve content based on results (views, retention, engagement)



MUST-HAVES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

• Proven short-form video editing skills (you can produce polished reels end-to-end)

• Strong ability to repurpose long-form videos into multiple clips efficiently

• Strong English skills (for captions, hooks, and on-screen text)

• Strong design sense for clean, professional marketing materials (Canva or similar)

• Extremely organized: you can run a content calendar and deliver on deadlines

• Tech-savvy and fast with tools and workflows

• Reliable and consistent (you hit deadlines every week)

• Must be able to work 11am–3pm EST, Monday–Friday



NICE-TO-HAVE

• Experience in mortgages, real estate, finance, or other professional services

• Comfortable turning raw talking points into hooks/scripts

• Familiarity with scheduling tools (Meta Business Suite, Later, Buffer, etc.)

• Experience creating client-facing resources (guides, checklists, explainers)



YOUR PORTFOLIO IS REQUIRED

To be considered, you MUST submit:

• Your resume

• A portfolio link that includes:

Short-form video edits (Reels/TikTok/Shorts)

Examples of repurposed clips from long-form content

Marketing materials/graphics you created (flyers, guides, branded assets)

• A short note confirming you can work 11am–3pm EST (M–F)



IMPORTANT: Applications without relevant video editing samples and marketing material samples will not be reviewed.

title: Studio Project Manager — Savage Forms
description:
We’re hiring a project manager who will run the day-to-day of our studio — the kind of person who thrives on keeping complex projects moving, clients informed, and every detail accounted for.



About Savage Forms

Savage Forms works on high-end residential and hospitality spaces that feel collected, not decorated — designed, precise, and sourced with intention. Our work spans high end residential renovations in Sagaponack and Miami to ambitious hospitality builds in Costa Rica and Atlanta.



We move fast, think globally, and care deeply about execution. We’re building a studio where great ideas don’t get lost in chaos — and that’s where you come in.



About the Role

You’ll be the operational backbone of Savage Forms — the person ensuring 10+ concurrent projects stay organized, on schedule, and moving forward.



This is a project management role first, with light design support where helpful. You will not be responsible for CAD, modeling, or technical drawings — we have dedicated specialists for that. But ability to easily modify CAD drawings, or relevant design softwares are a big +++/



You’ll coordinate people, timelines, decisions, and communication — while helping translate design ideas into clear, polished presentations that clients can confidently approve. You'll prep client presentations, gather specified products to present, run budgets and track deadlines.



If you love structure, communication, and making complex projects feel seamless, this role is for you.



What You’ll Own

Project Management & Operations

Manage timelines across multiple projects using tools like ClickUp and Gantt charts

Keep deliverables, decisions, and dependencies on track

Proactively identify risks, bottlenecks, and gaps — and solve them before they escalate

Maintain organized project documentation and workflows



Client Communication & Leadership

Serve as a primary point of contact for clients across multiple projects

Run weekly check-ins and keep communication clear, proactive, and organized

Translate design updates into clear next steps and decisions

Build trust by being responsive, thoughtful, and detail-oriented



Vendor & Procurement Coordination

Source materials and vendors globally (we’ll guide you on this)

Request quotes, track orders, and manage procurement timelines

Coordinate between vendors, designers, and contractors to ensure alignment



Construction & Execution Support

Coordinate with GCs and external architects on timelines and deliverables

Track progress and ensure materials and decisions are aligned with project schedules

Help keep projects moving smoothly from concept through execution



Design Support (Light)

Create polished presentations, mood boards, and client decks (Canva preferred)

Support early-stage design thinking through research, references, and organization

Help translate ideas into visuals that clients can understand and approve

Photoshop or visual editing skills are a plus



Studio Operations

Help the studio run smoothly day-to-day

Track expenses, maintain files, and improve systems

Identify ways to streamline workflows and increase efficiency



What You D

This is a high-trust role. You’ll have real input on:

How projects are structured and managed

Tools, systems, and workflows we use

Vendor coordination processes

How we communicate with clients and run projects day-to-day



Who You Are

3–6+ years managing projects (design, construction, hospitality, or similar fields preferred)

Exceptionally organized — you can manage multiple moving pieces without losing clarity

Strong communicator — clear, calm, and professional with clients and teams

Proactive — you don’t wait to be told what’s broken

Detail-oriented without losing sight of the big picture

Comfortable working remotely and independently

Interested in design, even if you’re not a technical designer



Must-Have

Proven project management experience

Background in Architecture, Interior Design or Graphic Design

Strong written and verbal English

Experience managing timelines, stakeholders, and deliverables

Comfort with tools like ClickUp, Google Workspace, or similar



Nice-to-Have

Canva and/or Photoshop skills (for presentations and light design work)

Experience in interior design, architecture, or construction environments

Exposure to procurement or vendor coordination

International experience or sourcing familiarity

Spanish or another second language



Compensation & Benefits

$6/hr-$9/hr

Fully remote (Americas/Europe time zones preferred)

Flexible schedule with core weekly touchpoints

Generous PTO + holiday closures

Real opportunity to grow into a leadership role



How to Apply

Send a short video + résumé to 
 us about a project where you brought order to complexity and made things run better. Keep it short — 150 words is plenty.

title: Studio Architect — Savage Forms
description:
About Savage Forms

Savage Forms works with high-end residential and hospitality spaces that feel collected, not decorated — designed, precise, and sourced with intention. Our work stretches from high-touch multi-million residential renovations in Sagaponack and Miami to ambitious hospitality builds in Costa Rica and Atlanta.

We believe an architect's best ideas show up when they're trusted to run their own ship. So we hire people who want exactly that — and give them the autonomy, the budgets, and the mentorship to actually pull it off.



About the Role

You'll be the creative and technical pulse of Savage Forms — the architect who turns vision into built reality, quickly and carefully. You'll make mood boards, sketch early layouts, co-run intake meetings, coordinate with external architects and GCs, source tile in three time zones, and build presentations that make clients say yes.

If you just want to be told what to do, this isn't the role. If you want to build a studio around your own creative standards — your ideas, your systems, your creative vocabulary — you're exactly who we're looking for.



What You'll Own

Design & Drafting: Draft initial layouts in CAD, create 3D models (SketchUp/ARCHICAD), mood boards (Canva/Adobe Suite), creative concepts, lighting plans, installation plans, and elevation details of custom millwork and custom furniture. Material takeoffs, collaborate with external architects. Lead schematic through CD on smaller residential projects.



Client Leadership: Co-run weekly design catch-ups and intake meetings. Lead client conversations on design direction. Be the trusted creative voice clients rely on — whether one wants arched windows or another wants the laundry room rethought entirely.



Vendor & Procurement: Source materials internationally — zellige from Morocco, stair fabricators in New York, impact doors and windows in Miami, furniture restoration experts. Request bids, compare quotes, negotiate, and manage orders end to end. We'll provide the roadmap, support, and mentorship.



Construction Coordination: Partner with GCs to review shop drawings, ensure design intent is maintained on site, and keep projects on schedule and on brand.



Brand & Presentation: Build polished client decks and mood boards in Canva. Represent Savage Forms with the same care you'd bring to your own firm — in writing, in meetings, on Zoom.



What You D

You will have real creative input and authority. This role isn't advisory. You d
:

- Which design directions make the final cut

- Which vendors we partner with long-term for materials and fabrication

- How to respond when a client, a contractor, or a municipality throws a curveball





Who You Are

- A working architect (licensed or on the path), with 3–6+ years running projects

- Confident and warm with clients — you can walk someone through a floor plan, a cost tradeoff, and a permitting delay in the same call, and they thank you for it

- Thorough on construction administration — submittals, RFIs, and change orders are second nature

- Curious about materials, sourcing, and craft — you have opinions about tile, and you're glad we asked

- Excited by creative freedom and disciplined enough to use it well





Must-Have

- Fluency in architectural drafting software (ARCHICAD / AutoCAD / SketchUp / Rhino — show us what you love)

- Portfolio that includes residential and/or hospitality work

- Experience working with contractors and municipalities

- Excellent written English



Nice-to-Have

- Licensure, or on a clear path to it

- International procurement experience

- A second language (Spanish is especially useful to us)

- Interior design sensibility — we live at the intersection of architecture and interiors

- Canva and Adobe Suite fluency



Growth Path

This role reports directly to our founder, who will be your first mentor, sounding board, and co-conspirator.

- Year 1. You own the design and technical output of the studio.

- Year 2. You're shaping creative strategy — vendor partnerships, design direction, client selection.

- Year 3. You're operating as a principal, with a stake in the studio's future.





Compensation & Benefits

- Fully remote — work from anywhere in a compatible time zone (we have teammates in the Americas and Europe)

- Flexible schedule anchored by a few recurring weekly touchpoints

- Generous paid time off plus company-wide closure on major holidays

- A real shot at creative leadership — this isn't theater



How to Apply

- Send a short video + portfolio + résumé to 
 Tell us about a project where you took real creative initiative and made something better. No cover-letter essay needed — 150 words is plenty.



We read every application and reply to everyone within one week.

Savage Forms is an equal-opportunity employer. We hire for talent, curiosity, and craft — not background, identity, or pedigree. If you'd do this job well but don't hit every bullet, apply anyway.

title: Payroll Specialist
description:
Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable (AR/AP) Specialist



Location: 100% Remote

Department: Finance & Accounting

Reports To: Accounting Manager / Controller



Position Summary



The Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable (AR/AP) Specialist is responsible for managing and executing day-to-day billing, collections, and vendor payment activities for a medical staffing organization. This role ensures accurate invoicing of healthcare clients, timely processing of clinician-related payables and vendors, and compliance with contractual terms, healthcare regulations, and internal accounting controls.



Key Responsibilities



Accounts Receivable (AR)



* Prepare, review, and submit client invoices based on timesheets, contracts, and bill rates for healthcare staffing assignments

* Reconcile timekeeping records with billing data to ensure accuracy

* Manage collections process, including follow-up on outstanding invoices and resolving client billing discrepancies

* Post and apply cash receipts, adjustments, and credits accurately

* Maintain detailed AR aging reports and escalate delinquent accounts as needed

* Coordinate with recruiters, payroll, and client services to resolve billing issues



Accounts Payable (AP)



* Process vendor invoices, clinician reimbursements, and operating expenses in accordance with company policies

* Verify invoice accuracy, approvals, and proper coding to general ledger accounts

* Schedule and process weekly or biweekly payment runs

* Maintain vendor records, W-9s, and payment terms

* Reconcile AP sub-ledger to the general ledger



Financial Controls & Reporting



* Assist with month-end close, including AR/AP reconciliations and accruals

* Ensure compliance with contractual billing terms and internal controls

* Support audits by providing documentation and explanations as requested

* Identify process improvements to increase efficiency and accuracy



Qualifications



* 5+ years of AR/AP or accounting experience, preferably in medical staffing, healthcare, or professional services

* Strong understanding of invoicing, collections, and AP processes

* Experience working with high-volume transactions and time-based billing

title: Website Copywriter — AI-Assisted, Direct Response, SEO & Conversion Focused
description:
Website Copywriter — AI-Assisted, Direct Response, SEO & Conversion Focused



Remote | Full-Time or Part-Time

Work Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST



---



PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING



This is not a role for blog writers, academic writers, or anyone who thinks website copy is about filling pages with words.



We are looking for a Website Copywriter who understands:



- How to write website copy that makes people take action — not just read

- Direct response principles — headlines, hooks, CTAs, objection handling, urgency

- SEO copywriting that sounds like a human wrote it — meta titles, meta descriptions, heading hierarchy, keyword integration, search intent, local SEO, internal linking, image alt text

- How Google actually ranks content now — natural language, topical depth, and relevance. Not keyword stuffing. If you still think SEO means cramming the same phrase into every paragraph, stop here.

- How to use AI writing tools (Claude, ChatGPT) to draft faster, then rewrite and humanise the output until it sounds like a real person who knows what they are talking about

- How to work inside Figma — placing and editing copy within actual page designs, not just handing over a Google Doc



If you can write website copy for service-based businesses that ranks, converts, and sounds like it was written by someone who actually understands the business — keep reading.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO



You will write website copy for service-based businesses across trades, health, real estate, professional services, events, and more. Every page you write has two jobs: rank in search and convert the person who lands on it.



You will work within our AI-assisted workflow. You will use Claude, ChatGPT, and our internal prompts to generate drafts and frameworks, then rewrite, sharpen, and humanise the output. The final copy must read like a sharp human wrote it — not a machine.



You will work directly inside Figma. You place your copy into the actual website designs so the build team works from one source of truth instead of cross-referencing separate documents.



Here is what the day-to-day looks like:



- Write homepage, service page, about page, landing page, and location page copy for client websites

- Write above-the-fold value propositions — the headline, subheadline, and CTA a visitor sees before they scroll

- Write benefit-driven body copy that speaks to what the customer wants, not just what the business does

- Write objection-handling copy — anticipating why someone might hesitate and addressing it before they leave

- Write risk-reversal copy — guarantees, free quotes, no-obligation language that lowers friction

- Write social proof sections — testimonial intros, case study summaries, credibility stacks (certifications, awards, years in business)

- Write CTAs that actually say something — not just "Contact Us" but specific, benefit-driven actions that match the page

- Write micro-copy — form labels, placeholder text, button text, thank you pages, confirmation messages

- Write FAQ sections that handle objections, build trust, and support schema markup

- Write SEO-optimised meta titles and meta descriptions for every page — click-worthy, within character limits, and aligned with search intent

- Write heading hierarchy properly — one H1 per page, logical H2 and H3 structure that reflects what the searcher is looking for and what Google needs to understand the page

- Write keyword-mapped copy that integrates primary and secondary keywords naturally — like a normal person talking about the topic, not like someone trying to game an algorithm

- Write image alt text that is descriptive and accessible, not stuffed with keywords

- Write internal linking anchor text — connecting service pages, location pages, and supporting content with natural, relevant anchors

- Write URL slug recommendations — short, clean, keyword-relevant

- Write location and service area pages for local SEO — suburb-specific, city-specific pages that are genuinely useful and unique. Not the same page with the suburb name swapped out.

- Write to search intent — you know whether a page needs to answer a question, compare options, or drive an action, and you structure the copy accordingly

- Write content that can target featured snippets — structured lists, direct answers, table-format content where it fits

- Structure FAQ and review content to support schema markup

- Use AI tools to draft faster — then rewrite everything until it reads naturally and sounds specific to the business

- Work inside Figma to place copy directly into designs, not in a separate doc

- Adapt tone and voice across industries — a plumber's website does not sound like a cosmetic clinic

- Turn around copy quickly and accurately — this is a fast-paced agency, not a leisurely content mill



---



OUR APPROACH TO SEO COPY



This is important, so read it carefully.



We do not keyword stuff. Ever.



Google uses natural language processing to understand content. It reads context, synonyms, related terms, and topical depth. It does not count how many times you jammed a keyword into a paragraph.



We expect you to understand this. We expect you to write copy that covers a topic thoroughly and naturally — the way a genuine expert in that field would explain it to a potential customer. That is what ranks. That is what converts. That is what we do.



If you still write SEO copy by repeating exact-match phrases in every heading and every second sentence, this role is not for you.



What we expect:



- You understand that Google rewards topical authority, not keyword repetition

- You write naturally and let keywords sit where they belong — in headings, in the first paragraph, in anchor text — without forcing them

- You cover related subtopics, synonyms, and questions a real searcher would have

- You structure content so Google can understand it (heading hierarchy, FAQ structure, clear topic flow) without making it read like it was written for a crawler

- You know the difference between writing for the person searching and writing for the search engine — and you know the best SEO copy does both at the same time



---



MUST-HAVE EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)



- Proven experience writing website copy for service-based businesses — homepage, service pages, landing pages, about pages, location pages. Not blogs. Not social captions. Not product descriptions.

- Direct response copywriting skills — you can write a headline that stops someone, a subheadline that pulls them deeper, body copy that handles objections, and a CTA that gets them to act.

- SEO copywriting that sounds human — you can integrate keywords naturally, write proper meta titles and descriptions, structure headings correctly, write internal linking anchors, and build location pages that are genuinely unique. You understand search intent and you write to it.

- A modern understanding of how Google ranks content — you know that natural language, topical depth, and content quality matter more than keyword density. You do not stuff keywords. Full stop.

- Experience using AI writing tools — you have used Claude, ChatGPT, or similar. You can prompt well, get useful output, and then rewrite it until it sounds like a human who knows the subject.

- Figma proficiency — you can navigate Figma, find the right frames, and place or edit copy directly in website designs. You do not need to be a designer but you need to be comfortable in the tool.

- Excellent written English — native or near-native level. Grammar, spelling, punctuation, and sentence structure must be flawless.

- Ability to adapt tone and voice — you can write for a tradesperson and a luxury brand on the same day without the copy sounding identical.

- Self-editing discipline — you proofread your own work before submitting. You do not rely on someone else to catch mistakes.



If your experience is only blogs, social media, or academic writing — this is not the right fit.

If you copy-paste AI output without rewriting it — this is not the right fit.

If your idea of SEO is repeating a keyword as many times as possible — this is not the right fit.



---



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience writing for Australian businesses or audiences

- Familiarity with GoHighLevel landing pages

- Understanding of CRO principles — heatmaps, A/B testing insights, scroll depth

- Experience with keyword research tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Ubersuggest) or working from keyword briefs

- Familiarity with NLP-related SEO concepts — entity salience, TF-IDF, topical relevance scoring

- Basic understanding of WordPress site structure (pages, posts, Yoast/RankMath fields)

- Experience writing Google Business Profile descriptions and local citation copy

- Understanding of schema markup (FAQ, Local Business, Review) and how copy structure supports it

- Experience using Grammarly, Hemingway Editor, or similar tools

- Experience writing
 sequences, thank you page copy, or post-enquiry follow-up flows



---



THIS ROLE IS NOT A FIT IF



- You only write blog posts or social media content

- You copy-paste AI output and submit it as finished work

- You have never written a homepage or service page for a real business

- Your SEO approach is stuffing keywords into every sentence

- You do not understand meta titles, heading hierarchy, or search intent

- You cannot work inside Figma

- Your English writing has consistent grammar or spelling issues

- You write the same generic copy for every industry

- You need heavy hand-holding on every page



THIS ROLE IS A FIT IF



- You can write a homepage that makes someone pick up the phone

- You understand the difference between features and benefits

- You know why a service page and a location page need different copy even when they describe the same service

- You understand that good SEO copy reads naturally — because that is what Google rewards

- You use AI to move faster, not to avoid thinking

- You are comfortable working directly inside Figma

- You take ownership of what you deliver — clean, sharp, and ready to go live



---



WHY WORK WITH US



- Work directly with the founder — no middle management

- No micromanagement — clear briefs, clear ownership, clear accountability

- Long-term clients across multiple industries — stable, varied workload

- AI-assisted workflow — access to Claude, ChatGPT, and our internal prompts to help you work faster

- Fast-moving agency that values speed and quality equally

- No-fluff, performance-focused environment

- Remote role with consistent hours and weekly pay



---



HOURS AND PAY



- Remote role (Philippines preferred)

- Working hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST

- Weekly pay via Wise

- Part-time or full-time (based on performance and workload)

- Long-term opportunity for the right person



---



WORK SETUP REQUIREMENTS



Please confirm the following when you apply:



- Internet speed: Minimum 25 Mbps download / 10 Mbps upload (stable wired connection preferred)

- Monitors: Dual monitors required — you will be working across Figma, AI tools, briefs, and reference material at the same time

- Backup internet: Must have a backup connection (mobile hotspot or second ISP) for outages

- Availability: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST, Monday to Friday. Must be available and responsive during these hours.

- Power backup: UPS or alternative power solution for brownouts if applicable to your area

- Workspace: Dedicated, quiet workspace free from distractions



---



HOW TO APPLY



To be considered, please send:



1. A short intro on your website copywriting experience — which industries, what types of pages, and your approach to SEO. How do you balance keyword targeting with natural, readable copy?

2. 2–3 live links to websites where you wrote the copy. Tell us which pages are yours and point out any SEO elements you handled (meta titles, heading structure, location pages, etc.)

3. A short sample task — Write the following for a fictional Perth-based plumbing company called "Reliable Plumbing Perth" targeting the keyword "emergency plumber Perth": (a) A homepage H1 headline and subheadline, (b) A meta title and meta description, (c) An opening paragraph (above the fold), (d) One FAQ question and answer that could support FAQ schema. Use AI to generate your first draft, then rewrite and humanise it. Submit both versions so we can see your process. If the sample reads like keyword-stuffed SEO content, your application will not move forward.

4. Your work setup — number of monitors, internet speed (screenshot of a speed test), backup internet, power backup

5. Your timezone and daily availability

6. Include the word CONVERSION in your application so we know you read this far



Applications without live website copy examples and the completed sample task will not be reviewed.



---



FINAL FILTER



This role is for copywriters who write pages that rank and convert — not just pages that exist.



If you know why a meta title is not the same as an H1, if you understand that Google reads content like a human now, if you can take an AI draft and turn it into something that sounds like a person who actually gives a damn — you will do well here.



If you write for the person searching and not just the search engine, and you self-edit before you hit send — you will thrive.

title: UGC & Project Manager
description:
What this position is responsible for:



We’re looking for a highly organized and proactive UGC & Project Manager to oversee creator relationships and manage the operational flow of UGC campaigns. This role plays a critical part in ensuring that creator partnerships, campaign deliverables, and internal collaboration run smoothly from start to finish. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced digital marketing environment, is detail-oriented, and can confidently coordinate between creators, internal teams, and clients to ensure campaigns are executed at a high standard.



Requirements/Experience:

- 1–3+ years of experience in UGC management, influencer marketing, social media marketing, or digital marketing operations



- Experience sourcing, recruiting, and managing UGC creators or influencers across platforms such as TikTok and Instagram



- Strong project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously



- Excellent communication and negotiation skills when working with creators and coordinating with internal teams



- Ability to manage timelines, deliverables, and campaign documentation with a high level of attention to detail



- Familiarity with social media trends, creator ecosystems, and UGC-style content



- Experience reviewing content and ensuring it follows brand guidelines, scripts, and campaign briefs



- Proficiency with project management tools, spreadsheets, and communication platforms (e.g., Google Sheets, Notion, Slack, Trello, etc.)



- Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to identify risks, delays, or bottlenecks early



- Self-starter mindset with the ability to work independently in a remote environment





Roles & Responsibilities for this position:



UGC Management



- Source and recruit high-quality UGC creators across platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, and other relevant social media channels to support ongoing campaigns.



- Build, maintain, and continuously grow a reliable roster of creators, ensuring access to talent that aligns with brand positioning and campaign goals.



- Conduct creator outreach and relationship management, including communication, negotiation of deliverables, and maintaining professional partnerships.



- Distribute campaign briefs, scripts, and brand guidelines to creators, ensuring all expectations and creative requirements are clearly communicated.



- Review and evaluate creator submissions to ensure content meets campaign standards before forwarding for internal or client approval.



- Track and document creator performance, reliability, and content quality to inform future collaborations.



- Maintain and organize creator databases, ensuring accurate records of contact details, past performance, and campaign participation.



Project Management



- Coordinate campaign timelines and deliverables between creators, internal team members, and clients to ensure smooth campaign execution.



- Monitor campaign schedules to ensure all deliverables are completed on time and meet required deadlines.



- Organize and maintain campaign assets and documentation, including briefs, scripts, and project files.



- Track overall project progress, proactively identifying potential delays, bottlenecks, or issues that may impact timelines.



- Communicate updates across teams to maintain alignment and ensure all stakeholders are informed throughout the campaign lifecycle.



- Support the overall campaign workflow by ensuring tasks, deadlines, and responsibilities are clearly defined and executed.



Apply today to join our team!

My 
 is "repaholic" Feel free to add me right away with your portfolio, and I’ll DM you back if interested.



Thank you!

title: Experienced Shopify Web Developer- Liquid, HTML, CSS, JS
description:
We are an American home décor and textiles brand - we sell fabric, wallpaper, furniture, vintage pieces, and accessories through our Shopify store. We're a small, remote team, mainly based in the USA and we work in a relaxed, collaborative way. We value people who communicate clearly, take initiative, and get things done to a high standard without needing to be chased.



We're looking for an experienced Shopify developer with excellent spoken and written English to help us improve and maintain our store on an ongoing basis. You'll work closely with our Shopify coordinator and one of our co-founders.  This is a part-time, long-term role — we're not looking for a one-off job, we're looking for someone to become a trusted part of the team.\



 WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

- Solid Shopify theme development experience (Liquid, HTML, CSS, JS)

- Experience with Shopify Broadcast theme. 

- Experience consolidating product variants and setting up colour swatches — this one is essential

- Strong understanding of Shopify collections, tags, metafields, and filtering

- Able to customize metafields.

- Excellent spoken and written English. 

- Able to diagnose issues, not just execute tasks

- Clear and patient communicator — you'll be working with a non-technical tea
mbers day to day

- Experience with Shopify apps, installing and managing them, liaising and problem solving with Shopify App developers

- RELIABLE, proactive, and honest about what's possible.

- Able to work 2-4 hours per day between the hours of 9am and 3pm USA Eastern Time. 



Nice to have:

- Experience with trade/wholesale apps on Shopify.

- Comfortable making short screen-recorded tutorials for other members of staff to view



How to Apply:  Please don't just send your CV. In your application, tell us:

We will invite selected high-caliber candidates for video interview and to complete a short task to demonstrate their skills.

title: Virtual Assistant - Operations & Inventory
description:
WHO WE ARE

LuxuryLink Group is a fast-growing international luxury goods trading business. We operate fully remote across multiple time zones, move quickly, and hold our team to a high standard. We’re building out our operations team and need someone who can keep up — and make us better.



THE ROLE

You’ll own the back-end of our operations: inventory tracking, shipments, financial bookkeeping, and documentation. Every item we buy needs to be tracked from purchase through delivery without gaps or errors — that’s the baseline.

What will set you apart is what you bring beyond execution. We want someone who has worked in fast-moving e-commerce or sourcing environments, understands what good ops looks like, and isn’t shy about suggesting a better way to do things. If you’ve built a tracker, introduced a process, or automated something repetitive at a previous job — we want to hear about it.

You’ll work closely with our General Manager and have direct visibility with the founder.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Inventory & SKU Tracking

• Log all purchased items with full details (SKU, brand, cost, landed cost, selling price, supplier, shipment batch)

• Reconcile purchased vs shipped vs received items

• Flag mismatches and missing items immediately

• Keep the inventory tracker clean and current daily



Shipment & Logistics

• Track every shipment from supplier through to client

• Update status daily; escalate delays fast

• Follow up with suppliers on missing or incomplete orders

• Own the full pipeline: ordered ? in transit ? received ? delivered ? paid



Financial Bookkeeping

• Record purchases and expenses into financial trackers (QuickBooks)

• Ensure books match inventory and shipment logs

• Organize invoices and payment records

• Weekly buying and expense summaries



Documentation & Process

• Maintain photo records organized by SKU via Google Drive

• Build and improve templates, trackers, and SOPs

• Suggest and implement better ways to do things

• Keep the founder’s visibility into the pipeline automatic, not manual



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Must-Haves

• Highly organized and detail-oriented — this role is 90?curacy

• Strong Google Sheets / Excel skills (VLOOKUP, basic formulas)

• Ability to track multiple shipments and SKUs simultaneously without errors

• Strong follow-up instincts — you chase, you don’t wait to be asked

• Fluent written English



Strong Plus

• Background in e-commerce, sourcing, logistics, or inventory management

• Experience with QuickBooks or similar bookkeeping tools

• Exposure to automation tools (Zapier, Google Apps Script, Airtable, Notion)

• Experience in a fast-moving small business where you had to figure things out



Personality Fit

• You flag problems before they become fires

• You look at a messy process and immediately think of a cleaner way

• You work independently — no hand-holding needed

• Reliable means reliable: if you say it’s done, it’s done

• You communicate clearly when something is wrong



COMPENSATION

$750–$1,000 USD/month depending on experience, paid reliably every month. Performance-based incentives available once you’ve established yourself in the role — we reward people who raise the bar.



TOOLS

Google Sheets / Excel · Google Drive · QuickBooks Online · WhatsApp / WeChat /
 · Shipment tracking portals · Any automation tools you bring to the table



GROWTH PATH

This role has a direct path to a full Operations Lead position as the business scales — including supplier performance tracking, dispute handling, and owning the full ops function end-to-end. We promote based on results, not tenure.



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, send a message through this platform with your resume and a short note covering: what ops or admin work you've done before, a process or system you've built or improved, and how you stay organized across multiple shipments or orders. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a short video call. Applications without a note will not be reviewed.

SKIP Supplements is a fast growing DTC e-commerce brand from the Netherlands. We combine festivals and health with one mission: help people enjoy the weekend without sacrificing the days after. We are a young, fast moving team that values clear processes, quick communication, and high quality work.



Role overview

We are looking for a part time Senior Graphic Designer focused on Meta Ads. The founder currently designs everything, and we need an experienced designer to take over production so we can scale faster. Success after 90 days means you consistently deliver 10 static ad creatives per week, on brand and ready to launch.



You will receive clear briefings and copy. You do not need to write copy, but you must understand English at B2 level so you can follow briefings and feedback.



Responsibilities

Weekly

Design 10 static Meta ad creatives based on our briefs and templates

Prepare export files in the correct sizes and formats for Meta

Keep designs consistent with our brand guidelines and improve them over time



Monthly

Help us build and improve a simple design system, templates, and reusable components



Requirements

Strong portfolio with DTC performance creatives, preferably Meta ads

Experience designing static ad creatives that are clear, punchy, and conversion focused

Ability to work fast without losing quality, consistent weekly delivery matters

B2 level English to understand briefings and feedback

Good file organization, naming, and version control

Proactive communication, you ask questions early instead of guessing





Tools you will use

Figma or Adobe XD for design

Notion for briefs, planning, and asset delivery

Slack, WhatsApp, or
 for communication

Google Drive for files, if needed



Working hours and communication

You can choose your own working hours. We work in Amsterdam time, but the role is fully async.



Pay and growth

This is a part time role. We expect the workload to be around 8 hours per week. The pay is $5 per hour. We are a fast growing brand, so if things go well, there is a strong chance we will increase your weekly hours quickly.



How to apply

Application questions

Due to the high number of applications, we can’t respond to everyone individually. If we’d like to move forward with your application, you’ll hear from us. If you don’t hear from us, we won’t be taking your application further.

title: WordPress Website Designer and Builder (Bricks Builder)
description:
WordPress Website Designer and Builder (Bricks Builder)



Remote | Full-Time or Part-Time

Work Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST



---



PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING



This role is not for theme installers, Elementor-only builders, or people who use pre-made templates and call it web design.



We are looking for a WordPress Website Designer and Builder who can do both — design clean, modern websites in Figma and then build them accurately in WordPress using Bricks Builder.



You need to be strong at:



- Designing website pages and layouts in Figma — not just wireframes, actual high-fidelity designs with proper spacing, typography, and visual hierarchy

- Building those designs in Bricks Builder — translating Figma to a live site that looks exactly like the design, not "close enough"

- Responsive implementation — desktop, tablet, and mobile must all look intentional, not just squeezed down

- Working from briefs and direction — you will receive page structures, copy (from our copywriter), and brand guidelines. Your job is to make it look great and build it properly.

- Working fast without cutting corners — this is an agency environment with multiple client projects running at the same time



If you can design a website that looks like it was done by a proper studio and build it in Bricks so it loads fast, works on every device, and matches the design exactly — keep reading.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO



You will design and build WordPress websites for service-based businesses across trades, health, real estate, professional services, events, and more. Every site you produce needs to look professional, load fast, and be built to convert visitors into leads.



You will work from briefs, brand guidelines, and copy provided by our team. Sometimes you will receive a full Figma design to build from. Other times you will design the pages yourself in Figma based on a brief, get approval, and then build. You need to be comfortable with both workflows.



Our copywriter handles all website content. Your job is to make that content look great on the page — the visual hierarchy, the layout, the spacing, the typography, the overall feel. Copy will either be placed directly into Figma designs for you or provided separately for you to integrate during the build.



Here is what the day-to-day looks like:



Design



- Design website pages and layouts in Figma based on provided briefs, page structures, and brand guidelines

- Create clean, modern designs with strong visual hierarchy — a visitor should know exactly where to look and what to do on every page

- Maintain consistent spacing, typography, colour usage, and component patterns across every page of a site

- Design with conversion in mind — the layout should guide the visitor toward the CTA, not just look pretty

- Design responsive layouts — you think about how the page works on tablet and mobile during the design phase, not as an afterthought

- Apply brand guidelines accurately — colours, fonts, logo usage, imagery style

- Design reusable components and sections that can be used across multiple pages and client sites

- Present design work for review and iterate based on feedback quickly



Build



- Build approved designs in WordPress using Bricks Builder — the live site must match the Figma design accurately

- Implement responsive behaviour across desktop, tablet, and mobile — every breakpoint should look intentional and polished

- Keep builds clean, lightweight, and well-structured — no bloated code, no unnecessary plugins, no messy class naming

- Optimise for page speed — image compression, lazy loading, minimal CSS/JS, clean markup. Sites need to score well on PageSpeed Insights.

- Implement on-page SEO fundamentals as directed — heading hierarchy (H1, H2, H3), image alt text, clean URL structures, schema-ready markup

- Set up and configure essential plugins — forms, analytics, caching, security, SEO (Yoast or RankMath)

- Build reusable templates and global elements in Bricks — headers, footers, section templates that can be maintained centrally

- Test thoroughly before handover — cross-browser testing, mobile testing, form testing, link checking, speed testing

- Troubleshoot and fix issues — if something breaks or does not look right, you own it and fix it without being chased



General



- Manage multiple client website projects at the same time without dropping quality or missing deadlines

- Communicate clearly on progress, blockers, and timelines — you flag issues early, not after a deadline passes

- Follow SOPs and checklists for design and build processes

- Stay current with Bricks Builder updates, WordPress best practices, and modern web design trends



---



MUST-HAVE EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)



- Proven experience designing websites in Figma — not just wireframes or mockups. Full page designs with proper typography, spacing, visual hierarchy, and responsive considerations. You must have a portfolio showing this.

- Strong WordPress build experience using Bricks Builder specifically — you know how to use Bricks to build custom layouts, work with conditions, create templates, and structure sites properly. If your only experience is Elementor or Divi, this is not the right role.

- Ability to translate a Figma design to a live WordPress site accurately — what you design is what you build. No "close enough." Pixel-level attention to detail.

- Strong responsive design skills — you build for desktop, tablet, and mobile deliberately. Not just letting things stack and hoping it looks fine.

- Understanding of page speed optimisation — you know how to keep a WordPress site fast. Image optimisation, minimal plugin usage, clean code, caching basics.

- Understanding of basic on-page SEO implementation — heading hierarchy, image alt text, clean URLs, meta fields. You do not need to be an SEO specialist but you need to implement the fundamentals correctly.

- Understanding of basic CRO and UX principles — you design and build pages that guide visitors toward an action. You think about visual hierarchy, whitespace, CTA placement, and user flow.

- Experience designing and building websites for service-based or lead-generation businesses — not just ecommerce, not just personal blogs.

- Ability to work from briefs and direction — you receive a brief, brand guidelines, and copy, and you produce a polished result. You do not need to be told every detail but you follow the structure and direction given.

- Ability to manage multiple projects at once without dropping quality or missing timelines.

- Self-motivation and accountability — you do not wait to be chased. You flag issues, meet deadlines, and take ownership of your work.



If your experience is only Elementor or Divi — do not apply.

If you only design but do not build — do not apply.

If you only build but cannot design in Figma — do not apply.

If your portfolio is only personal projects or template customisations — do not apply.



---



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience with other WordPress builders (Zion Builder, Oxygen, Breakdance) in addition to Bricks

- Experience with WooCommerce or membership site builds

- Basic PHP, CSS, or JavaScript knowledge for custom tweaks beyond what Bricks provides out of the box

- Experience with GoHighLevel landing page builds or embeds

- Experience working with a copywriter or content team — receiving copy and integrating it into designs rather than writing it yourself

- Familiarity with Google Tag Manager, conversion tracking setup, or analytics implementation

- Experience creating Loom walkthroughs of your design or build process

- Animation and interaction design experience — tasteful micro-interactions, scroll-triggered animations, hover states

- Experience with website accessibility basics (WCAG)



---



THIS ROLE IS NOT A FIT IF



- You only use Elementor or Divi

- You only design or only build — not both

- You rely on pre-made templates and just swap content

- Your builds are slow, bloated, or plugin-heavy

- You are not comfortable working in Figma

- You cannot build responsive sites that look good on every device

- You prefer heavy strategy or client-facing project management — this is a design and build role, not a strategist role

- You are uncomfortable working from briefs and direction

- You need to be chased for updates or deadlines



THIS ROLE IS A FIT IF



- You are strong in Figma and confident designing full website pages from a brief

- You can build exactly what you design in Bricks Builder

- You care about clean execution — pixel-level accuracy, fast load times, responsive on every device

- You work well from clear instructions and structures

- You can manage multiple builds at once without things falling apart

- You want to focus on design and build, not strategy or project management

- You take pride in your work and own the quality of every site you deliver



---



WHY WORK WITH US



- Work directly with the founder — no middle management

- No micromanagement — clear briefs, clear ownership, clear accountability

- You will have a dedicated copywriter providing all website content — you focus on design and build, not writing

- Long-term clients across multiple industries — stable, varied workload

- Fast-moving agency that values speed and quality equally

- No-fluff, performance-focused environment

- Remote role with consistent hours and weekly pay



---



HOURS AND PAY



- Remote role (Philippines preferred)

- Working hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST

- Weekly pay via Wise

- Part-time or full-time (based on performance and workload)

- Long-term opportunity for the right person



---



WORK SETUP REQUIREMENTS



Please confirm the following when you apply:



- Internet speed: Minimum 25 Mbps download / 10 Mbps upload (stable wired connection preferred)

- Monitors: Dual monitors required — you will be working across Figma, Bricks Builder, and reference material simultaneously

- Backup internet: Must have a backup connection (mobile hotspot or second ISP) for outages

- Availability: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST, Monday to Friday. Must be available and responsive during these hours.

- Power backup: UPS or alternative power solution for brownouts if applicable to your area

- Workspace: Dedicated, quiet workspace free from distractions



---



HOW TO APPLY



To be considered, please send:



1. A portfolio showing websites you both designed and built — we need to see your Figma designs alongside the live sites. If you only show live sites with no evidence of the design work, your application will not be reviewed.

2. 3–5 live links to websites you personally designed and built. Tell us which pages are yours and what builder was used.

3. A short summary of your experience with Figma and Bricks Builder — how long you have used each, what types of sites you have built, and your typical workflow from design to live site.

4. Your current work setup — number of monitors, internet speed (screenshot of a speed test), backup internet, power backup

5. Your timezone and daily availability

6. Include the word BLUEPRINT in your application so we know you read this far



Applications without real examples of both design and build work will not be considered. Screenshots of Figma designs with matching live site links are what we want to see.



---



FINAL FILTER



This role is for designer-builders who care about craft — not people who install a theme and move things around.



If you can take a brief, design something clean and conversion-focused in Figma, and then build it in Bricks so it loads fast, looks identical to the design, and works perfectly on every device — you are exactly who we are looking for.



If you take pride in the details — the spacing, the typography, the hover states, the mobile experience, the load time — and you do not ship work you are not proud of, you will do well here.

title: Credit Repair Dispute Specialist

HOW TO APPLY:

 with your resume attached and a brief overview of your relevant experience in the body of the

title: CRM & Tech Automation Specialist
description:
We're one of the fastest-growing agencies in the education space — we've driven $15M+ in sales for our clients in just 1.5 years, and we're scaling fast. We're looking for a CRM and tech expert who wants to grow with us, not just clock in and clock out.



This is not a "just do my tasks and log off" role. If that's what you're looking for, please don't apply. We want an innovator — someone who spots problems before they're reported, suggests better ways to do things, and takes genuine ownership of the systems they manage.



What you'll be doing: 

- You'll be the tech and automation backbone for two client accounts, responsible for:

- Managing and optimizing CRM workflows, automations, and integrations across GoHighLevel (GHL) and Close CRM

- Responding to bug/error notifications within 5–10 minutes and resolving them with precision

- Obsessing over the details — small issues don't get ignored, they get fixed properly

- Proactively identifying weak points in our systems and improving them before they break

- Building automations that save time and scale our operations



Who we're looking for:

- Hands-on experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) and Close CRM is required — please don't apply without it

- Proven track record building automations, workflows, and integrations

- Fast, detail-obsessed, and accountable — you don't let things slip through the cracks

- Strong problem-solver who actually enjoys digging into technical issues

- Someone with an owner's mindset — you treat the systems like they're your own

- Excellent written English and reliable communication

- Able to work the required schedule consistently



Schedule & Compensation:

- 6 days/week (Sunday–Friday), 40 hours total

- $1,000/month starting pay

- Clear pathway to raises and performance bonuses as you contribute and grow with the company



The long-term opportunity: We're building one of the biggest agencies in the education space. The right person here won't stay at $1,000/month — they'll grow into a senior role with compensation to match. We invest in people who invest in us.



How to apply: 

- Send an
 to 
 with:

1. A Loom video introducing yourself, walking through your GHL and Close CRM experience, and explaining why you specifically would be a great fit for this role

2. Any relevant portfolio links or examples of automations/systems you've built

**Applications without a Loom will not be reviewed.**

title: Accounting Manager
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking a Senior Accounting Manager to lead and orchestrate the financial operations of a large-scale, global enterprise. This is not a "hands-on" role for a generalist; it is a strategic leadership position requiring a candidate who has mastered the complexities of high-volume transactions and multi-national entity management.



The successful candidate will oversee a sophisticated accounting department, providing high-level coordination across specialized functions including AP, AR, Cost Accounting, and FP&A. You will be responsible for the integrity of the group’s financial health, specifically focusing on the consolidation of multiple overseas sub-entities and ensuring seamless financial reporting at scale.



Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic Leadership & Team Management

-Directly manage and mentor a team of functional managers (AP Manager, AR Manager, Cost Manager, and FP&A Manager).

-Standardize accounting workflows across departments to ensure maximum efficiency and accuracy.

-Act as the primary bridge between technical accounting and strategic financial planning.



2. Global Consolidation & Reporting

-Lead the month-end and year-end closing process for the parent company and 3+ international sub-entities.

-Prepare and review Consolidated Financial Statements in accordance with IFRS/GAAP, ensuring all intercompany eliminations and foreign currency translations are flawlessly executed.

-Oversee global audit coordination and ensure compliance with international statutory requirements.



3. Operational Excellence & Oversight

-Supervise the Cost Management function to ensure manufacturing/operational costs are optimized and accurately reflected.

-Collaborate with the FP&A Manager to align historical accounting data with forward-looking budgets and forecasts.

-Ensure the AR and AP functions are operating at scale, maintaining healthy cash flow and vendor/client relationships.



4. Systems & Internal Controls

-Maintain and improve internal control frameworks (SOX or equivalent) within a large-scale ERP environment (SAP, Oracle, etc.).

-Drive continuous process improvement to handle increasing transaction volumes and organizational complexity.



Qualifications & Requirements

-Experience: Minimum of 10+ years of progressive accounting experience, with at least 3 years in a Senior Management role within a large corporate environment or a Big 4 accounting firm.



-Scale Mastery: Proven track record of managing accounting for organizations with significant revenue and complex departmental structures. Experience in small-to-mid-sized firms alone will not be sufficient for this role.



-Technical Expertise: Expert-level knowledge of Consolidated Financial Statements and multi-currency reporting.



-Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA or CMA certification is highly preferred.



-Systems: Advanced proficiency in Tier-1 ERP systems (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle EBS) and consolidation software.



-Leadership: Demonstrated ability to manage other managers and influence stakeholders at the executive level.

title: Commercial Kitchen Equipment Plan - Florida DBPR Submission (Project-Based)
description:
Simmer Down Bar & Grill needs to UPDATE our existing indoor equipment plan to add new outdoor cooking equipment and revised seating arrangements for Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) compliance.

What We Currently Have:



Existing DBPR-approved equipment plan for indoor kitchen at 517 25th Street, West Palm Beach, FL 33407

Current indoor kitchen layout and equipment schedule on file with DBPR



What We Need Updated:

1. ADD OUTDOOR EQUIPMENT to existing plan:



Outdoor grill/jerk pit (specifications and placement)

Oil disposal system (used cooking oil storage and disposal)

Outdoor trash receptacles (location and specifications)

Grease trap (new or updated specifications and location)

All outdoor equipment properly positioned and dimensioned

Utility connections for outdoor equipment (gas, electric, water, drainage)



2. UPDATE Indoor Seating Chart: 2 circular 4 seaters and 3 circular 2 seaters with 6 bar stools at counter 



Revised seating layout and count

Updated table and counter configurations

Current occupancy calculations

Updated floor plan showing new arrangement



3. UPDATE Counter/Service Area:



Any changes to counter layout

Updated dimensions and flow

Service area modifications



IMPORTANT - This is NOT an hourly job:



We need a fixed price quote for updating the existing plan

Payment upon completion and approval

No hourly billing - quote the entire update project



Deliverables Required:

? Updated equipment layout drawing showing indoor + outdoor equipment (CAD format)

? Updated equipment schedule with all new outdoor items listed

? Updated seating chart with occupancy count

? Updated utility/plumbing plan showing outdoor connections

? Grease trap specifications and location (existing or new)

? Oil disposal system details

? Editable files plus final PDFs ready for DBPR submission

?  revisions included in price

Specific Outdoor Equipment to Add:

Outdoor Cooking:



Commercial grill/jerk pit (we'll provide specifications or need recommendations)

Gas connections and/or electrical hookups

Proper clearances and fire safety compliance



Waste Management:



Used cooking oil disposal container (specify type, size, location)

Outdoor trash receptacles (number, size, placement per code)

Grease trap (new installation or modification to existing)



Utilities:



Charcoal outdoor grill

Electrical connections None Needed

Water supply if needed

Drainage for grease trap



Ideal Candidate Must Have:

REQUIRED:



Experience updating existing Florida DBPR equipment plans

Knowledge of Florida health code and food service regulations (Chapter 61C-4, FAC)

Portfolio showing DBPR plan updates that were approved

Understanding of outdoor cooking equipment regulations

Experience with grease trap sizing and placement per Florida code

AutoCAD or professional kitchen design software proficiency



HIGHLY PREFERRED:



Experience with outdoor kitchen additions to existing restaurants

Knowledge of Palm Beach County health department requirements

Understanding of oil disposal regulations and equipment

Caribbean/ethnic restaurant experience (jerk pits, outdoor grills)



Must Understand:



How to modify existing DBPR-approved plans properly

Outdoor cooking equipment requirements and codes

Grease trap regulations and sizing for outdoor additions

Oil disposal system requirements

Outdoor waste management regulations

Fire safety and clearance requirements for outdoor grills



Timeline: 1 week from project start to final updated deliverable

Project Details:



Current Location: 517 25th Street, West Palm Beach, FL 33407



Type: Indoor Jamaican restaurant adding outdoor grill/jerk pit at new location

We have: Existing DBPR-approved indoor equipment plan at 517 25th Street (will provide to hired contractor)

Need: Updated plan showing all additions for DBPR amendment/update

title: Go High Level Funnel & Automation Specialist
description:
Description:



This a contract based role to start that has the potential to move into part/full time depending on performance. We are not looking for someone to “build a funnel.”



We are looking for someone who understands how a business actually runs.



Someone who sees the full picture, traffic, conversion, automation, follow-up, and can connect all of it into a clean, functional system that works without constant babysitting.



If you’ve spent years building funnels, mapping workflows, and setting up automations that actually convert, keep reading.



About Us:



We are a faith-centered health company focused on helping perimenopausal women who are navigating hormone-related challenges.



Our systems matter. Our messaging matters. And how everything connects behind the scenes matters even more.



We are building something long-term, structured, and scalable, not patchwork marketing.



What You’ll Be Responsible For:



• Building and optimizing funnels inside Go High Level

• Mapping out full marketing flows, from first touch to conversion to follow-up

• Creating and refining automations, pipelines, and CRM systems

• Ensuring all systems are clean, organized, and actually usable by the team

• Supporting campaign execution and ongoing system improvements

• Identifying gaps and proactively solving them before they become problems

• Creating reporting systems that show performance of campaigns 



What We’re Looking For:



You’re not just a task-doer.



You think in systems.



You understand how traffic, funnels, and automation all connect. You don’t just “build pages”, you build processes that convert and scale.



You likely have:



• Strong experience with Go High Level (funnels, automations, CRM, pipelines)

• A solid understanding of marketing flow strategy and customer journeys

• Experience with SEO and/or traffic generation

• The ability to work independently and take initiative

• A problem-solving mindset, you don’t wait to be told what’s broken



Bonus if you have:



• Experience supporting coaches, agencies, or service-based businesses

• Familiarity with Monday.com & Google Workspace

• Basic knowledge of paid ads or outreach systems



What This Role Is NOT:



This is not a “click here, do this” type of role.



If you need step-by-step instructions for everything, this will not be a fit.



We are looking for someone who can step in, assess, improve, and build with minimal oversight.



To Apply:



Send a message with:



A short intro about your experience with Go High Level and automation

Examples of funnels or systems you’ve built

A brief explanation of how you approach building a marketing workflow from start to finish



If you’re someone who takes pride in building clean systems, thinks strategically, and enjoys making things work properly, we’d love to connect.

title: Podcast Strategist & Manager
description:
The Podcast Strategist & Manager is responsible for ensuring each weekly podcast episode is well planned, high quality, and positioned to perform strongly across YouTube and social media.







This role combines content strategy, quality control, performance testing, and podcast management, with the goal of helping each episode reach the right audience and perform at its highest potential.







Key Responsibilities



1. Pre-Production Strategy



This role begins before the episode is filmed.







Responsibilities include:



Researching each guest and identifying relevant angles and themes

Preparing the structure of the conversation

Developing topic direction and guiding questions

Identifying key talking points aligned with audience interest

Ensuring the episode is strategically positioned for strong content performance





2. Content Quality Control



After recording, the Podcast Strategist & Manager reviews the first edited draft to ensure:



The conversation flows well

Key 
nts are clear and engaging

The episode meets our quality standards

The content is structured for audience retention and clarity





3. Promotion & Growth Strategy



This role plays an active part in maximizing the reach and performance of each episode.



Responsibilities include:



Identifying strong 
nts that can be repurposed into short-form clips

Testing topics, hooks, and content angles

Supporting title and thumbnail testing

Working with the ads specialist to promote episodes ahead of launch

Ensuring each episode is set up for visibility and growth across platforms





4. Publishing & Distribution



The Podcast Strategist & Manager is responsible for ensuring all content is organized and published on time.



Responsibilities include:



Uploading and scheduling the full episode

Uploading and scheduling promotional clips

Ensuring titles, descriptions, and assets are optimized

Maintaining consistency in publishing timelines





Role Summary



This role sits at the intersection of strategy, content performance, and execution. The focus is on ensuring each podcast episode is thoughtfully planned, well produced, and positioned to achieve strong reach and engagement across platforms.







Role Details



Full-time

10am – 6pm SAST

Fully remote

title: Bookeeper / billing specialist
description:
Job Title

Staff Accountant (QuickBooks)



Employment Type

Full-Time (40 hours per week)



Location

Remote – Philippines



Compensation

USD $4–$6 per hour (approximately $800–$1,100 per month, depending on experience)



Role Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented Staff Accountant with hands-on experience using QuickBooks to support day-to-day accounting operations. This role is responsible for transactional accounting, reconciliations, invoice review, and maintaining accurate financial records. The position includes auditing vendor invoices—primarily from hospitals—to ensure accuracy and compliance before billing sponsors.



This is a long-term, full-time role within a growing finance team, offering stability and process-driven work.



Key Responsibilities

Vendor Invoice Review & Audit (Critical Function)

Review and audit vendor invoices (primarily hospitals) for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with contractual or sponsor requirements



Verify billed amounts against supporting documentation, rates, and services rendered



Identify discrepancies, duplicates, or errors and escalate issues for resolution



Prepare and organize approved vendor invoices for sponsor billing



Accounts Receivable (AR)

Prepare and issue sponsor and client invoices in QuickBooks



Bill sponsors based on approved and audited vendor invoices



Apply customer payments accurately and timely



Track client deposits, prepayments, and retainers



Monitor outstanding AR balances and flag past-due accounts



Accounts Payable (AP)

Enter vendor bills and expenses into QuickBooks



Track approval status of vendor invoices



Prepare payment schedules and record vendor payments



Ensure proper expense coding and documentation



Reconciliations

Perform monthly bank account reconciliations



Reconcile credit card statements and resolve discrepancies



Investigate and correct transaction variances



General Accounting & Controls

Maintain accurate, well-organized financial records



Ensure transactions are coded correctly and consistently



Support month-end close activities as assigned



Follow established accounting procedures, audit trails, and internal controls



Required Qualifications

2+ years of accounting or bookkeeping experience



Strong working knowledge of QuickBooks (Online or Desktop)



Experience with:



Accounts Receivable and sponsor/client billing



Accounts Payable and vendor invoice processing



Auditing or reviewing vendor invoices for accuracy



Bank and credit card reconciliations



Client deposits and prepayments



High attention to detail and strong analytical skills



Ability to follow documented processes and checklists



Reliable internet connection and ability to work full-time



Preferred Qualifications

Experience auditing invoices from hospitals, healthcare providers, or large institutions



Experience supporting U.S.-based companies



Familiarity with billing sponsors, reimbursements, or pass-through costs



Exposure to healthcare, clinical research, or regulated environments



Work Style & Expectations

Detail-oriented and process-driven



Clear written communication in English



Proactive in identifying billing issues or discrepancies



Comfortable working with structured approval workflows and documentation



Able to work independently in a remote environment



Instrucitons to apply - please place favorite hobby in subject line

title: Delivery Executive
description:
Send your resume to 



WFH

Graveyard Shift

1 year contract required



***Role Overview

The Delivery Executive serves as the “Architect of Execution,” acting as the second-in-command responsible for translating the company’s strategic vision into actionable, scalable operational workflows. This role partners closely with the CEO to ensure the organization has the right people, processes, and systems in place to achieve sustainable growth and operational excellence.



***Key Responsibilities



Strategic Execution: Translate the CEO’s vision and company goals into clear, structured, and executable operational plans across all departments.



Operational Leadership: Oversee day-to-day operations, including service delivery, production, customer success, and internal support functions such as HR and IT.



Performance Management: Develop, implement, and monitor KPIs and OKRs to ensure teams consistently meet or exceed performance targets.



Financial Oversight: Work closely with the Finance team to manage budgets, optimize costs, and ensure efficient allocation of resources aligned with growth objectives.



Culture & Change Management: Lead organizational improvements and transformation initiatives while promoting a culture of accountability, performance, and continuous development.



Process Optimization: Identify operational inefficiencies and implement streamlined or automated solutions to improve productivity and service quality.



Cross-Functional Collaboration: Ensure alignment and seamless coordination between departments to support business objectives and enhance overall execution.



Client & Delivery Excellence: Maintain high standards of service delivery, ensuring client satisfaction, retention, and consistent operational performance.



***Qualifications



- Minimum of 5 years of experience in a senior leadership or operations role, preferably in a high-growth environment.



- Strong understanding of operational strategy, business processes, and organizational scaling.



- Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive measurable results.



- Experience in performance management frameworks (KPIs, OKRs).



- Financial acumen with experience in budgeting and resource planning.



- Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills.



- Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with a data-driven mindset.



-Industry experience in [e.g., BPO, SaaS, Logistics, etc.] is highly preferred.



***Success Metrics (First 12 Months)



- Achieve measurable efficiency improvements (e.g., reduce operational costs or increase productivity by at least 15%).



- Successfully scale operations within a key department or market segment.



- Improve service delivery performance and client satisfaction metrics.



- Maintain or improve employee retention and engagement levels.



- Implement at least one major process improvement or automation initiative.



Reporting Line

Reports directly to the CEO.

Job Summary:



We are seeking a highly organized and client-focused Account Delivery Manager to oversee the successful delivery of services and solutions to our key clients. This role acts as a bridge between the client and internal teams, ensuring that  service are delivered on time, within scope, and to the highest standard. The ideal candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and excels at managing client relationships while driving internal execution.



***Key Responsibilities:

- Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned client accounts, ensuring client satisfaction and retention.
